title: Register a payment against one or more invoices/bills
reuse_advice: Use the account.payment.register wizard (Register Payment) across selected invoices; it handles reconciliation. Don't create account.payment + reconcile by hand.
when_not_enough: a custom settlement engine with external clearing

title: Add data / react when a journal entry or invoice posts
intents: phản ứng khi xác nhận hóa đơn
reuse_advice: Extend account.move._post (call super()) — but the move is immutable after, so set data before super() returns. Don't hook the action_post shell or write() a posted move.
when_not_enough: changing how the line is built from a source doc (then the source doc's _prepare_invoice_line)

title: Tax computation and tax mapping (taxes, fiscal positions)
reuse_advice: Configure account.tax + fiscal positions; always compute via account.tax.compute_all (handles price_include, rounding, mapping). Never reimplement tax arithmetic.
when_not_enough: a jurisdiction with logic no tax/fiscal-position config can model (then a localization)

title: Prevent edits/posting in closed periods (lock dates)
reuse_advice: Use the native lock dates (Accounting settings / company) — posting/editing in a locked period is already rejected. Don't add custom date-guard code on account.move.
when_not_enough: per-user or per-journal locking beyond the standard company lock dates

title: Credit notes / reversing a posted entry
reuse_advice: Use the native reversal (Add Credit Note / account.move.reversal wizard) or account.move._reverse_moves. Don't hand-build an opposite-sign journal entry.
when_not_enough: a correction scheme your jurisdiction defines differently from a standard reversal
